repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: linked_editing_range was still doing most of the capability boilerplate itself.

Solution: refactor it to make use of the common Capability framework for handling enabling, disabling, etc.

comment_data.body: Is this handled by `vim.lsp._capability`? Or maybe this logic just isn't necessary anymore?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -49,25 +43,6 @@ local function clear_ranges(bufnr, client_state)
   client_state.range_index = nil
 end
 
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:detach(client_id)
-  local client_state = self.client_states[client_id]
-  if not client_state then
-    return
-  end
-
-  --TODO: delete namespace if/when that becomes possible
-  clear_ranges(self.bufnr, client_state)
-  self.client_states[client_id] = nil
-
-  -- Destroy the LinkedEditor instance if we are detaching the last client
-  if vim.tbl_isempty(self.client_states) then
-    api.nvim_del_augroup_by_id(self.augroup)
-    LinkedEditor.active[self.bufnr] = nil
-  end

comment_data.body: if this is a semantic tokens bugfix, maybe it should be done in a separate pr?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -802,7 +802,7 @@ function M.start(bufnr, client_id, opts)
 
   M.enable(true, { bufnr = bufnr, client_id = client_id })
 
-  if opts and opts.debounce then
+  if opts and opts.debounce and STHighlighter.active[bufnr] then

comment_data.body: this seems like a helpful example/reason to keep this logic, is it no longer true/necessary?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1195,7 +1196,7 @@ function Client:on_attach(bufnr)
   self:_run_callbacks(self._on_attach_cbs, lsp.client_errors.ON_ATTACH_ERROR, self, bufnr)
   -- schedule the initialization of capabilities to give the above
   -- on_attach and LspAttach callbacks the ability to schedule wrap the
-  -- opt-out (such as deleting the semanticTokensProvider from capabilities)

comment_data.body:
do we no longer need to return early in this case? maybe it's guaranteed to be impossible by the capability module?

we still check whether the state exists in the detach function, I'd assume there are no guarantees when attaching either?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end

comment_data.body: Yeah, this was in my working tree and accidentally got added. I'll move it out.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -802,7 +802,7 @@ function M.start(bufnr, client_id, opts)
 
   M.enable(true, { bufnr = bufnr, client_id = client_id })
 
-  if opts and opts.debounce then
+  if opts and opts.debounce and STHighlighter.active[bufnr] then

comment_data.body: the way to opt-out isn't by deleting the provider, it's just by enabling/disabling in the attach callback. I can just change the comment
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1195,7 +1196,7 @@ function Client:on_attach(bufnr)
   self:_run_callbacks(self._on_attach_cbs, lsp.client_errors.ON_ATTACH_ERROR, self, bufnr)
   -- schedule the initialization of capabilities to give the above
   -- on_attach and LspAttach callbacks the ability to schedule wrap the
-  -- opt-out (such as deleting the semanticTokensProvider from capabilities)

comment_data.body: This is all handled by the capability module now.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end

@@ -49,25 +43,6 @@ local function clear_ranges(bufnr, client_state)
   client_state.range_index = nil
 end
 
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:detach(client_id)
-  local client_state = self.client_states[client_id]
-  if not client_state then
-    return
-  end
-
-  --TODO: delete namespace if/when that becomes possible
-  clear_ranges(self.bufnr, client_state)
-  self.client_states[client_id] = nil
-
-  -- Destroy the LinkedEditor instance if we are detaching the last client
-  if vim.tbl_isempty(self.client_states) then
-    api.nvim_del_augroup_by_id(self.augroup)
-    LinkedEditor.active[self.bufnr] = nil
-  end

comment_data.body: In that case, can we just `assert` that the client state exists in the detach function? Unless that isn't part of the capability module guarantees
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end

comment_data.body: nit: i would personally prefer `client_states` (plural) since this var still holds references to multiple states, and we iterate over them below. it looks a bit odd to have `for client_state in client_state`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end
-  self.client_states[client_id] = {
-    namespace = api.nvim_create_namespace('nvim.lsp.linked_editing_range:' .. client_id),
-    word_pattern = '^[%w%-_]*$',
-  }
-end
+---@field client_state? table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>

comment_data.body: Good point, I'll do something similar to semantic tokens here and only initialize the client_state with a new state table if it didn't exist yet.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end

comment_data.body: maybe we should unconditionally run `self:refresh()` after initializing the client state? prior to this change, manually enabling linked editing ranges automatically checks for linked ranges below the cursor after. after this change, the user will have to move their cursor or change the text for a refresh to occur. i should've added a regression test for this behavior, apologies!
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -189,124 +164,38 @@ function LinkedEditor:refresh()
   end)
 end
 
----Construct a new LinkedEditor for the buffer.
----
----@private
----@param buf integer
----@return vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
-function LinkedEditor.new(buf)
-  local self = setmetatable({}, { __index = LinkedEditor })
-
-  self.bufnr = buf
-  local augroup = api.nvim_create_augroup('nvim.lsp.linked_editing_range:' .. buf, { clear = true })
-  self.augroup = augroup
-  self.client_states = {}
+---@package
+---@param client_id integer
+function LinkedEditor:on_attach(client_id)

comment_data.body: `client_state` is initialized and used by the base Capability class, so we can't change it. However, the loop you mention below is actually wrong. The nvim_on should only be attaching an event that runs for that particular client state on that particular buffer.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end
-  self.client_states[client_id] = {
-    namespace = api.nvim_create_namespace('nvim.lsp.linked_editing_range:' .. client_id),
-    word_pattern = '^[%w%-_]*$',
-  }
-end
+---@field client_state? table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>

@@ -189,124 +164,38 @@ function LinkedEditor:refresh()
   end)
 end
 
----Construct a new LinkedEditor for the buffer.
----
----@private
----@param buf integer
----@return vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
-function LinkedEditor.new(buf)
-  local self = setmetatable({}, { __index = LinkedEditor })
-
-  self.bufnr = buf
-  local augroup = api.nvim_create_augroup('nvim.lsp.linked_editing_range:' .. buf, { clear = true })
-  self.augroup = augroup
-  self.client_states = {}
+---@package
+---@param client_id integer
+function LinkedEditor:on_attach(client_id)

comment_data.body: actually, it looks like the capability module prefers `client_state` singular, so maybe when we loop over the editing range states we should just do `for linked_range_state in client_state`? up to you, this is all super nit-picky...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,24 +23,17 @@ local M = {}
 ---@field range_index? integer The index of the range that the cursor is on.
 ---@field namespace integer namespace for range extmarks
 
----@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor
+---@class (private) vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor : vim.lsp.Capability
 ---@field active table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.LinkedEditor>
----@field bufnr integer
----@field augroup integer augroup for buffer events
----@field client_states table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>
-local LinkedEditor = { active = {} }
-
----@package
----@param client_id integer
-function LinkedEditor:attach(client_id)
-  if self.client_states[client_id] then
-    return
-  end
-  self.client_states[client_id] = {
-    namespace = api.nvim_create_namespace('nvim.lsp.linked_editing_range:' .. client_id),
-    word_pattern = '^[%w%-_]*$',
-  }
-end
+---@field client_state? table<integer, vim.lsp.linked_editing_range.state>

comment_regular_data.body: @ribru17 lmk when this looks good to you and I'll merge.

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: refactor(lsp): refactor linked_editing_range to use vim.lsp._capability
